POST_PROMPT:
Create a natural, authentic recipe post in English that feels written by a real food blogger, not AI. CRITICAL: Vary your writing style, length, and structure for EACH recipe to appear authentically human-written. JSON STRUCTURE: { "title": "Natural recipe title (40-100 characters, vary the length): Authentic Styles (rotate between these): - Classic: Grandma's Chocolate Chip Cookies - Benefit: Easy 30-Minute Chicken Stir Fry - Descriptive: Creamy Garlic Parmesan Pasta - Simple: The Best Cornbread Recipe Rules: ✓ Sound natural, not SEO-stuffed ✓ Vary length: some short (40 chars), some longer (90 chars) ✓ Use conversational language", "promptIMG": "Food photography: [dish name], appetizing presentation, natural lighting, kitchen setting, documentary style, realistic colors --ar 4:3 --quality 2", "description": "Write 150-250 character natural description (VARY the length): Natural Formula: - What it is + why it's good - One special ingredient or technique - When to serve it Example: This creamy chicken pasta is comfort food at its best. Ready in 30 minutes with a rich garlic sauce. Perfect for busy weeknights! ✓ Write like texting a friend ✓ No formulaic structure ✓ Be genuine and specific", "hashtags": "3-5 relevant hashtags (VARY the number): #recipename #category #benefit Keep it simple and natural", "board_name": "Prioritize boards matching these high-affinity categories: Pizza, Sandwich, Soup, Salad, Seafood. Only use existing boards for other categories.", -"Pinterest board name optimized for SEO (2-5 words, Title Case): - Based on recipe category and main ingredient - Match what Pinterest users search for - Examples: \"Easy Chicken Recipes\", \"Healthy Soup Ideas\", \"Comfort Food Dinners\", \"Quick Weeknight Meals\" - NO hashtags, NO punctuation, just the board name", BOARD PRIORITY (by audience affinity): - Pizza recipes → affinity 16.3x (HIGH PRIORITY) - Sandwich ideas → affinity 14.5x (HIGH PRIORITY) - Soup recipes → affinity 10.9x - Salad recipes → affinity 7.8x - Seafood dinners → affinity 8.1x - Vegetable sides → existing strong board "pin_variations": [ // ⚠️ MANDATORY FORMAT — copy this exact pattern, only change the recipe-specific words: // Title 1 MUST start with "Why" and end with "?" // Title 2 MUST start with "What's the secret to" and end with "?" // Title 3 MUST contain "vs" and end with "?" // Title 4 MUST start with "Are you still" and end with "?" { "title": "Why Do Your [Vegetable/Dish] Always Turn Out [Bad Result]?", "description": "..." }, { "title": "What's the Secret to Perfectly [Adjective] [Dish]?", "description": "..." }, { "title": "[Method A] vs [Method B] — Which One Actually Wins?", "description": "..." }, { "title": "Are You Still Making [Dish] the Old-Fashioned Way?", "description": "..." } ] } Instructions: Create 4 pin variations. Every title must be phrased as a question that triggers curiosity or speaks directly to a pain point (e.g., "Why does...", "What if...", "Are you still...", "Did you know..."). Titles must be catchy, emotional, and optimized for Pinterest SEO (max 80 characters). Descriptions should be engaging, natural, and 2–3 sentences long — answer the question partially to create a curiosity gap, but don't reveal everything. Include a clear call-to-action (e.g., "Save this", "Try it today", "Discover now"). Add 4–8 relevant hashtags at the end of each description. Use 2–3 emojis per description naturally. Tone: inspiring, friendly, and scroll-stopping. Focus on keywords people search on Pinterest. Avoid repetition between variations (use different question angles: problem-question, curiosity-question, comparison-question, identity-question). "difficulty": "Easy|Medium|Hard", "prep_time": 15, "cook_time": 30, "servings": 4, "ingredients": [ "Clear measurements + ingredient + state", "In order of use when logical" ], "yield": "e.g. Approximately 6 cups, 4 servings", "introduction": "Write 2-4 paragraphs (150-250 words total) personal introduction about this recipe: - Open with your personal connection, backstory, or when you first made/discovered this dish - Explain what makes this version better or different from standard recipes - Mention 1-2 specific techniques or secrets that make it succeed - Set expectations: what the finished result tastes/feels/looks like ✓ First person, conversational tone ('I've been making this...', 'the trick I learned...') ✓ Include at least one honest detail (a past failure, a discovery, a family memory) ✓ Sound like a home cook sharing with a friend, not a recipe developer pitching", "instructions": [ { "step": 1, "instruction": "80-130 words: detailed action + technique explanation + WHY this step matters + what to watch for + common mistake to avoid at this step. Go beyond just listing what to do — explain what is happening and why it produces the right result.", "timing": "X minutes", "doneness_cue": "Specific visual, textural, or aromatic cue that this step is correctly done" }, { "step": 2, "instruction": "80-130 words: specific quantities + technique detail + temperature or timing explanation + what changes if you rush it or skip it.", "timing": "X minutes", "doneness_cue": "How to know this step is complete — be precise and sensory (color, sound, texture, smell)" } ], "why_this_recipe_works": { "section": "Natural section title like 'Understanding Why This Formula Succeeds' or 'the Science Behind This Recipe'", "content": "

3-4 detailed paragraphs (400-600 words total) explaining the science or technique that makes this recipe succeed. Cover ingredient interactions, cooking chemistry, temperature effects, Maillard reactions, emulsification, or whatever applies. Educational but accessible — explain the WHY behind each key step in depth.

behind it — make the reader understand WHY, not just WHAT" }, { "tip": "A third tip covering equipment, timing, or temperature", "reason": "70-100 words: detailed explanation with real consequences of not following it" } ], "common_mistakes_to_avoid": [ { "mistake": "Name the common mistake clearly", "why_it_happens": "50-70 words: explain the psychology or assumption behind why cooks make this error", "fix": "70-100 words: specific, actionable fix with exact technique, temperature, or timing correction" } ], "variations": [ { "name": "Variation name", "description": "80-110 words: how to adapt the recipe — ingredient swaps, technique changes, flavor profile shift, and what to expect differently in texture and taste", "technical_note": "40-60 words: technique adjustment, timing change, or texture difference to watch for" } ], "nutrition": { "calories_per_serving": "420 kcal", "protein_grams": "28g", "carbohydrates_grams": "35g", "fat_grams": "18g", "fiber_grams": "4g", "sodium_mg": "580mg", "note": "Nutritional values are estimates and may vary based on specific ingredients used." }, "storage_and_reheating": { "room_temperature": "How long it safely keeps at room temperature and how to cover/store it", "refrigerator": "Store in airtight container for up to X days. How to wrap properly, prevent freezer burn, thaw safely, and restore texture after freezing.", "reheating": "Specific reheating method (stovetop/microwave/oven), ideal temperature, time, what to add if needed (splash of liquid, etc.), and what to avoid", "make_ahead_strategy": "What steps can be done 1-2 days ahead, how to store the partially prepared dish, and what to do the day of serving" }, "faq": [ { "question": "A common question readers have about this specific recipe (troubleshooting, substitutions, timing, storage)", "answer": "80-120 words: specific, practical answer that genuinely solves the problem. Include the WHY, not just the WHAT. 8-10 FAQ entries required." }, { "question": "Another practical question about technique, ingredients, or results", "answer": "80-120 words: helpful, detailed answer based on real cooking knowledge" } ], "conclusion": "1-2 sentence warm closing. Invite readers to try the recipe and share results.", "seo": { "primary_keyword": "main 2-5 word search phrase for this specific recipe (e.g. 'southern pecan pie recipe')", "secondary_keywords": [ "related keyword phrase 1", "related keyword phrase 2", "how to make [recipe name]", "best [recipe name] recipe", "easy [recipe name]" ], "internal_links": [ {"text": "related recipe or technique text", "anchor": "anchor_slug_here"} ], "focus_keyword_density": "1.0-1.5%", "reading_level": "8th grade", "word_count_total": "approximately X words" } } TARGET AUDIENCE PROFILE: - American women, 55+ years old - Home cooks, not professional chefs - Cook for family, value simplicity - Prefer: classic comfort food, familiar ingredients - Avoid: trendy/exotic techniques, unusual ingredients - Tone: warm, practical, like advice from a neighbor CRITICAL AUTHENTICITY RULES: ✓ Use NATURAL language - write like a food blogger, not SEO copywriter ✓ Keyword density: 4-8 times MAXIMUM (not 10-15!) ✓ Imperfect is better - sound human, not AI ✓ Personal touches - "I love", "My family", "Works great when" ✓ Vary paragraph length - some 1 sentence, some 4 sentences ✓ Use contractions: "it's", "you'll", "don't" ✓ Occasional opinions: "I think", "in my experience" MANDATORY MINIMUM CONTENT PER FIELD (NON-NEGOTIABLE): ✓ "introduction": REQUIRED string, 250-350 words — NEVER null, NEVER omit this field ✓ "why_this_recipe_works": REQUIRED OBJECT (never a plain string!) with keys: - "section": section title string - "content": 400-600 words HTML string (3-4 paragraphs cooking science) - "ingredient_notes": object with 5-8 key ingredient names, each 70-100 words ✓ instructions: MINIMUM 9 steps, each step 100-150 words (include technique rationale, not just actions) ✓ pro_tips: 7-10 tips, each reason field 70-100 words of explanation ✓ common_mistakes_to_avoid: 5-7 mistakes, each fix 70-100 words ✓ variations: 5-7 variations, each description 80-110 words with technical_note ✓ storage_and_reheating: each field (room_temperature, refrigerator, freezer, reheating, make_ahead_strategy) 80-120 words ✓ faq: 8-10 Q&A pairs, each answer 80-120 words (troubleshooting, substitutions, timing) ✓ TOTAL: 4000-6000 words of actual content (ALL fields combined) NATURAL WRITING STYLE: ✓ First person occasionally: "I like to...", "I usually..." ✓ Second person casually: "You'll love...", "Try adding..." ✓ Mix sentence lengths ✓ Some shorter paragraphs (2 sentences) ✓ Some longer paragraphs (5 sentences) ✓ Transition words naturally: "Also,", "Plus,", "Another thing," ✓ Questions occasionally: "Want to make it spicier?" JSON VALIDATION (CRITICAL): ✓ Valid JSON only ✓ Double quotes for strings ✓ Escape quotes: \" ✓ Numbers without quotes: "prep_time": 15 ✓ No trailing commas ✓ Boolean: true/false (no quotes) ALLOWED HTML: ✓

    • ✗ No heading tags, div, span, or inline CSS INTELLIGENCE: ✓ Fill missing details logically ✓ Authentic techniques for cuisine type ✓ Realistic timing ✓ Appropriate suggestions ✓ Cultural accuracy Source Text: {SOURCE_TEXT}

Title rules: max 80 characters, ends in "?", emotional tone, includes 1 Pinterest SEO keyword from the recipe name. 6. The "title" of pin_variations[0] must NOT be identical to the top-level "title" field — they serve different purposes (top-level title is the blog post title; pin_variations titles are all questions). NEVER leave a question or answer field blank, empty, or with placeholder whitespace. ✓ TOTAL: 4000-6000 words of actual content (ALL fields combined) NATURAL WRITING STYLE: ✓ First person occasionally: "I like to...", "I usually..." ✓ Second person casually: "You'll love...", "Try adding..." ✓ Mix sentence lengths ✓ Some shorter paragraphs (2 sentences) ✓ Some longer paragraphs (5 sentences) ✓ Transition words naturally: "Also,", "Plus,", "Another thing," ✓ Questions occasionally: "Want to make it spicier?" ENCODING REQUIREMENT (CRITICAL): ✓ Output must use plain UTF-8 apostrophes and quotation marks only. ✓ NEVER output mojibake sequences like â€™, â€œ, â€ — if you intend an apostrophe, output a straight ' character, not an escaped or encoded variant. IMG_PROMPT_1:
A stunning ultra-premium food photograph of {title}, designed specifically to maximize visual craving, Pinterest saves, and instant scroll-stopping impact.

One hero portion of the dish dominates the foreground, positioned as the primary visual focal point. The most irresistible and appetite-triggering element of the recipe is prominently showcased, revealing rich textures, layers, freshness, and signature details that instantly communicate flavor and satisfaction.

Every visible ingredient is rendered with extraordinary realism and micro-detail. Crispy surfaces appear intensely crunchy, creamy elements look rich and velvety, sauces glisten naturally under the light, fresh ingredients appear vibrant and full of life, and layered components create visual depth and complexity.

Eye-level perspective, creating the sensation of sitting directly in front of the dish, ready for the first bite. The hero portion sits at the upper rule-of-thirds focal point while secondary elements softly recede into the scene, creating depth and visual storytelling.

Razor-sharp focus throughout the food. Every crumb, garnish, sauce streak, seasoning particle, texture variation, and surface detail remains tack-sharp and highly defined. Tiny realistic imperfections enhance authenticity and homemade appeal.

The food is elegantly plated on luxurious veined white marble. Warm directional side lighting sculpts every texture, creating appetizing highlights and dimensional shadows. Strong visual contrast between the dish and background ensures instant attention in a crowded Pinterest feed.

The food occupies most of the frame, emphasizing abundance and indulgence. The background melts into a creamy professional bokeh while the food remains perfectly sharp. No steam, no smoke, no fog, no clutter, no distracting props.

Photorealistic 8K detail. Editorial food magazine quality. Extreme texture realism. Rich appetizing colors. High dynamic range. Maximum craving appeal. Save-worthy Pinterest aesthetic. Scroll-stopping composition. Vertical 2:3 ratio. Slight Dutch tilt (2–3°). Luxury home kitchen atmosphere. Impossible to scroll past.

IMG_PROMPT_3:
A hyper-realistic extreme close-up food photograph of {title}, captured as if the camera is placed mere centimeters from the dish at near-table level — creating a powerful "you are right there" immersive perspective that pulls the viewer directly into the food.

Shot with a 100mm macro lens at f/5.6, ultra-tight framing where the food fills 95% of the frame. The perspective simulates the exact eye-level of someone leaning in, utensil in hand, about to take the first bite.

IRRESISTIBLE HERO DETAILS — automatically emphasize the most crave-inducing visual elements of this specific dish. Highlight its signature textures, ingredient layers, surface finishes, sauces, fillings, garnishes, freshness, caramelization, creaminess, crispness, juiciness, or any defining characteristic that makes this recipe uniquely appetizing.

Every microscopic surface detail is magnified and hyper-visible: ingredient textures, natural moisture, crispy edges, glossy sauces, creamy interiors, seasoning details, herb textures, and subtle reflections — all rendered at photographic maximum clarity.

Natural soft window light from the left side, wrapping gently around the food to create luminous highlights, realistic reflections, and soft micro-shadows that enhance depth, volume, and texture without harsh contrast.

The dish rests on a bright polished white marble surface, with only subtle reflections and minimal visible background elements, reinforcing luxury, cleanliness, and premium food photography aesthetics.

Minimal out-of-focus props at the extreme frame edges only when naturally relevant to the recipe. Nothing competes with the food.

Color palette optimized for maximum appetite appeal and Pinterest engagement, using the dish's natural ingredient colors to create strong visual contrast and immediate scroll-stopping impact.

Vertical 2:3 ratio. Macro immersion composition. The viewer's eye is naturally guided through the most visually appealing textures, layers, and details of the dish, creating depth, desire, and engagement.

STRICT NEGATIVE: no steam, no smoke, no fog, no CGI artifacting, no illustration style, no plastic-looking surfaces, no over-smoothing, no HDR halos, no text, no watermark, no logo, no unrealistic ingredients, no duplicated food elements, no background clutter.
